grant_cycles.notes: Call opened 9 April 2026 with €399.05 million budget, expected to fund ~1,600 projects. Deadline 9 September 2026. Researchers of any nationality can apply. PhD must be held by deadline. CRITICAL TIMELINE ISSUE: Researcher's PhD submission is October 2026 and defence Q1 2027 — PhD will NOT be awarded by the 9 September deadline. This is the single highest-priority opportunity but requires either (a) accelerating PhD submission to before 9 September 2026, or (b) waiting for the 2027 call (expected ~September 2027, too late for funding gap). New in 2026: resubmission restrictions apply for scores below 80%. Mobility rule: must not have resided in host country >12 months in 36 months before deadline. If applying to a non-Italy host, this is satisfied. If applying to Italy, NOT eligible (>12 months in Italy). Cardiff connection via Prof Huggins could be leveraged for a UK host.

grant_cycles.typical_deadline: February (annually, with internal university deadlines Nov-Dec prior year)
grant_cycles.notes: The 2026 round closed 19 February 2026. The next round (2027) is expected to open January 2027 with deadline February 2027. Internal university expressions of interest typically due November-December 2026. Awards are 3 years, ~145 fellowships available. ELIGIBILITY CONCERN: Applicants must either hold a degree from a UK HEI OR hold a UK academic position for ≥4 months prior to closing date. Researcher has NO UK degree and the Cardiff research visit (Aug 2025 – Jan 2026) may not qualify as an 'academic position'. This needs careful checking with the host university. PhD must be submitted for viva by the closing date — if February 2027, PhD submission Oct 2026 satisfies this if viva follows. The 2026 round introduced increased Leverhulme contribution (100% salary Y1 up to £56k, 50% Y2-Y3 up to £28k).

The ERC-2027-StG call is expected to open July 2026 with deadline October 2026. MAJOR CHANGE for 2027: eligibility window extended to 10 years post-PhD (up from 7 years), and eligible immediately after PhD defence (no 2-year minimum). Funding up to €1.5 million for 5 years, any nationality can apply. TIMELINE ISSUE: Researcher's PhD defence is expected Q1 2027, which is AFTER the October 2026 deadline. The researcher could apply if PhD is defended before the deadline, but current timeline makes this very tight. Best to plan for the ERC-2028-StG call (expected October 2027) as a medium-term target. Host institution must be in an EU Member State or associated country.

The Veni 2026 round pre-proposal deadline is expected early September 2026, with full proposals due January 2027. Veni grants are up to €320,000 for 3 years. Eligibility: PhD obtained within 3 years of the reference date (likely 1 January 2026). The researcher's PhD is expected Oct 2026/Q1 2027 — eligibility depends on whether PhD is obtained by the pre-proposal deadline. CRITICAL: Requires an 'embedding guarantee' from a Dutch research institution. The researcher's MSc from Groningen provides a Dutch network connection. Researcher would need to secure support from a Dutch university (e.g., University of Groningen Faculty of Spatial Sciences) well in advance. NWO is currently undergoing an evaluation of the Talent Programme expected to complete September 2026, which may result in changes.

The 2026-27 round opens July 2026 with outline deadline 30 September 2026. Fellowship is 3 years, starting Autumn 2027, tenable at UK universities. ELIGIBILITY: British citizens and EEA nationals eligible regardless of where PhD obtained. Anyone of any nationality with a UK PhD is eligible. Others may qualify with 'strong prior association' with the UK academic community. Researcher is Indonesian (non-EEA), no UK PhD, but has a research visit at Cardiff (Aug 2025 – Jan 2026). This MIGHT constitute 'strong prior association' — needs careful checking. Viva expected between 1 April 2024 and 1 April 2027 — researcher's expected Q1 2027 defence fits. IMPORTANT: Unsuccessful applicants cannot reapply — do not apply unless confident of a strong application. Success rate ~10%.

Full proposal deadline 1 June 2026 via DTPs. INELIGIBLE: Applicants must have completed their PhD at a UK research organisation that is part of ESRC's doctoral training network. Researcher's PhD is from Politecnico di Milano (Italy). This scheme is restricted to UK-trained PhD holders regardless of nationality.

grant_cycles.typical_deadline: Rolling (changing in 2026 due to system migration)
grant_cycles.notes: Typically for established academics, not directly for postdocs. The Trust is migrating its grants management system in 2026 — outline applications accepted into new system from September 2026. Not directly applicable but worth noting for future PI-led applications.

upcoming_deadlines.action_needed: HIGHEST PRIORITY. Call is NOW OPEN (since 9 April 2026). Must determine immediately whether PhD can be completed before 9 September 2026. If not possible, start identifying host institution and supervisor NOW for the 2027 call while exploring whether PolMi can accelerate the PhD process. Begin contacting potential host supervisors (e.g., Prof Huggins at Cardiff, or other European institutions). Draft research proposal outline immediately.

Outline applications due 30 September 2026. Start NOW: (1) Verify eligibility re 'strong prior association' with UK academia via Cardiff visit; (2) Contact potential mentor at a UK university; (3) Draft outline proposal. Internal university deadlines will be July-August 2026. Note: unsuccessful applicants cannot reapply — only apply if confident.

Pre-proposal expected September 2026. Start NOW: (1) Contact University of Groningen Faculty of Spatial Sciences about embedding guarantee; (2) Check eligibility calculator given PhD timing; (3) Draft narrative CV and research idea. Must secure institutional support before submission.

Call expected to open July 2026 with October 2026 deadline. New 10-year eligibility window applies. However, PhD must be defended BEFORE the deadline — if defence is Q1 2027, researcher is NOT eligible for this round. Monitor call opening; plan for ERC-2028-StG instead. Begin developing the research concept now.

The 2027 Leverhulme round is expected to open January 2027 with deadline February 2027. Internal university shortlisting typically November-December 2026. Begin contacting UK host departments NOW (Cardiff Geography, or other UK Geography/Planning departments). Clarify whether Cardiff research visit satisfies the UK connection requirement, or whether a UK degree requirement blocks eligibility.
NOT ELIGIBLE — requires UK PhD. No action needed.

strategy_recommendations: MSCA-PF IS THE TOP PRIORITY: The 2026 MSCA-PF call is now open (9 April 2026) with a 9 September 2026 deadline. This is the single best-matched programme: open to any nationality, any discipline, any host in EU/associated countries. The critical constraint is the PhD requirement — you must hold a PhD by the deadline. Investigate IMMEDIATELY with Politecnico di Milano whether you can submit and defend your thesis BEFORE 9 September 2026. Even a few weeks' acceleration could be career-defining.

DEVELOP A 'CARDIFF PATHWAY' FOR UK FELLOWSHIPS: Your research visit to Cardiff (Aug 2025 – Jan 2026) with Prof Robert Huggins is your key UK connection. For the British Academy PDF (outline deadline 30 Sept 2026), your Cardiff association MAY satisfy the 'strong prior association' criterion for non-UK/non-EEA applicants without a UK PhD — but this needs explicit confirmation from the BA. For the Leverhulme ECF (next round Feb 2027), you need either a UK degree or 4+ months in a UK academic position at the time of application deadline. A Cardiff research visit may not count as an 'academic position' under Leverhulme rules; investigate whether Cardiff can offer a visiting researcher title that qualifies.

PURSUE THE NWO VENI VIA THE GRONINGEN CONNECTION: Your MSc from the University of Groningen is a strong connection to the Netherlands. The Veni (pre-proposal expected September 2026) requires an embedding guarantee from a Dutch institution. Contact the Faculty of Spatial Sciences at Groningen NOW to explore whether they would support a Veni application. Your spatial microsimulation and regional economics expertise is well-aligned with Dutch economic geography research traditions (Groningen, Utrecht, Amsterdam). Note: PhD must be obtained within 3 years of reference date.

BUILD A PARALLEL APPLICATION STRATEGY — DO NOT PUT ALL EGGS IN ONE BASKET: Given your October 2026 PhD submission and Q1 2027 defence, many deadlines fall in an awkward gap. Prepare MSCA-PF 2026 (if PhD can be accelerated), BA PDF 2026-27, NWO Veni 2026, and Leverhulme ECF 2027 simultaneously. The research proposals can share a core project concept adapted to each scheme's requirements.

FRAME YOUR RESEARCH FOR MAXIMUM IMPACT: Your strongest unique selling point is the combination of (a) spatial microsimulation/digital twin methods with (b) applied policy evaluation in childcare/welfare (PNRR Piano Asili Nido) and (c) a developing country perspective (Indonesia). For European fellowships, frame a project that extends your Italian childcare microsimulation to a comparative European context (e.g., EU-SILC-based cross-country childcare policy simulation). For UK fellowships, emphasise the UK policy relevance (childcare expansion is also a UK priority under current government).

SECURE 2-3 STRONG REFERENCE LETTERS: You need letters from your PhD supervisor at PolMi, Prof Huggins (Cardiff), and ideally one more senior figure (e.g., Prof Carlos Mendez from Nagoya/QuaRCS Lab). Approach them now and keep them informed of your application timeline.

PUBLISH BEFORE DEADLINES: With 3 journal publications (2 as first/sole author in BIES and IRSA), your publication record is reasonable but could be stronger. Prioritise submitting at least one paper from your PhD thesis BEFORE September 2026. A paper under review can be listed on CVs and demonstrates an active pipeline.

PREPARE A BRIDGE PLAN IF THE FUNDING GAP MATERIALISES: If no fellowship is secured before October 2026 (PNRR expiry), explore (a) short-term postdoc positions at PolMi or another Italian university, (b) assegno di ricerca positions in Italy, (c) returning to Indonesia temporarily to apply from there (for MSCA-PF 2027, this could actually help satisfy the mobility rule for applying to European hosts).

emerging_opportunities.opportunity: UKRI strategic restructuring creating new cross-disciplinary programmes from spring 2026
emerging_opportunities.relevance: UKRI is moving to a new 'three buckets' model from April 2026, with £14.5bn for curiosity-driven research over 4 years and £8.3bn for strategic government/societal priorities. While some funding programmes have paused (MRC, BBSRC), ESRC funding continues as normal. New strategic programmes aligned with Industrial Strategy sectors are expected from spring 2026 onwards. This could create new opportunities in applied regional economics, particularly around place-based industrial strategy, levelling up, and spatial inequality — all core to the researcher's profile. Monitor UKRI Funding Finder regularly.

emerging_opportunities.opportunity: ERC eligibility window extended to 10 years for Starting Grants from 2027
emerging_opportunities.source: ERC news on changes to 2026 and 2027 Work Programmes (erc.europa.eu)
emerging_opportunities.relevance: From the ERC-2027-StG call onwards, researchers are eligible immediately after PhD defence and for 10 years (up from 2-7 years). This means the researcher will be eligible for ERC StG for a very long window after PhD defence (Q1 2027 through ~2037). This removes the previous 2-year post-PhD minimum requirement. Researcher should begin developing an ERC StG concept for the 2028 call (expected October 2027) — this is a realistic medium-term target.

emerging_opportunities.opportunity: ERC 'Choose Europe for Science' new instrument — substantial funding for up to 7 years
emerging_opportunities.source: ERC news on changes to 2026 and 2027 Work Programmes (erc.europa.eu)
emerging_opportunities.relevance: The ERC Scientific Council is developing a new funding instrument offering substantial funding for up to 7 years, as part of the 'Choose Europe for Science' initiative. Details are expected in an amended ERC Work Programme 2026. This could be a major new opportunity for ambitious researchers — monitor announcements.

emerging_opportunities.source: Kiel Institute for the World Economy (kielinstitut.de)
emerging_opportunities.relevance: A new postdoc program with the first cohort starting September 2026. Open to all nationalities, PhD in economics or related field within 3 years, or dissertation submitted by start date. Strong English required, German not required. Focus on geoeconomics and security. The 2026 intake deadline was November 2025 (missed), but future cohorts should be monitored. The program is based in Berlin or Kiel and embedded in a major economic research institute — good fit for regional/international economics.

emerging_opportunities.opportunity: Harvard Kennedy School 'Reimagining the Economy' Postdoctoral Fellowship
emerging_opportunities.relevance: 2-year postdoctoral fellowship at CID/Malcolm Wiener Center working with Gordon Hanson and Dani Rodrik on industrial policies, place-based policies, and productive development. JEL classifications include R1 (General Regional Economics) and O1 (Economic Development). The 2025-26 listing had a February 2026 deadline. Monitor for 2026-27 cycle listings (typically posted fall 2026). Excellent thematic match with researcher's interests.

emerging_opportunities.opportunity: NWO Talent Programme evaluation may introduce changes
emerging_opportunities.relevance: NWO is conducting a full evaluation of its Talent Programme (Veni, Vidi, Vici, Rubicon) expected to complete September 2026. This may result in adjustments to assessment criteria, success rates (target 25%), and the evidence-based CV format. Any changes would likely apply from the 2027 round onwards. The evaluation may create opportunities if criteria become more inclusive or if success rates improve.

cv_gaps_to_address: PUBLICATION OUTPUT: With 3 publications (2 sole-authored, 1 co-authored), output is respectable but below the competitive level for top fellowships. MSCA-PF and BA PDF both heavily weight the applicant's track record. ACTION: Submit at least one PhD chapter as a journal paper BEFORE September 2026. Target journals like Regional Studies, Journal of Regional Science, Papers in Regional Science, or Environment and Planning A/B. Even 'under review' status strengthens the CV significantly.

NO FIRST-AUTHORED PUBLICATION FROM PhD RESEARCH YET: Your PhD is on PNRR childcare expansion in Italy — this is a timely and policy-relevant topic. A first paper from this work would demonstrate that the PhD has generated novel contributions beyond the thesis itself. ACTION: Prioritise a short, policy-focused paper (e.g., for a letters journal or policy brief) on preliminary findings from the Italian childcare microsimulation.

LIMITED TEACHING EXPERIENCE: Many UK fellowships (BA PDF, Leverhulme ECF) expect fellows to contribute to teaching. Your CV does not mention teaching experience. ACTION: If possible, arrange to give guest lectures or lab sessions at PolMi or during future visits. Even a few hours of documented teaching strengthens the case.

GRANT/FELLOWSHIP APPLICATION TRACK RECORD: Competitive fellowships like to see evidence that the applicant can win funding. Your CV has no recorded fellowship applications or small grants won independently. ACTION: Consider applying for small travel/research grants (e.g., from IRSA, RSA, ERSA, or PolMi internal funds) before the main fellowship applications to build this track record.

CONFERENCE VISIBILITY: The CV does not list conference presentations. ACTION: Present at major conferences (RSA annual conference, ERSA Congress, RGS-IBG annual conference, AAG) in 2026 to build visibility and network with potential fellowship hosts/referees.

WEAK UK INSTITUTIONAL FOOTPRINT: For UK-based fellowships (BA PDF, Leverhulme ECF), the Cardiff visit (Aug 2025 – Jan 2026) is the sole UK connection and it has already ended. ACTION: Maintain active collaboration with Prof Huggins through co-authored work or continued involvement in Cardiff research events. If possible, arrange a return visit or a formal honorary/visiting appointment to strengthen the connection.

NO FORMAL COLLABORATION WITH EUROPEAN POLICY INSTITUTIONS: For MSCA-PF, demonstrating inter-sectoral mobility (e.g., a placement with a policy body) strengthens the application. ACTION: Seek connections with bodies like ISTAT, Eurostat, OECD territorial development division, JRC (Seville), or national planning agencies that align with your policy evaluation expertise.
